name: graphnosis
description: Your local, encrypted personal knowledge graph — recalled by any AI client.
long_description:
Graphnosis is a privacy-first personal memory system that runs entirely on your device. It stores your notes, decisions, projects, files, and conversations in a local encrypted graph, then makes that knowledge available to any AI client via MCP — so your AI always has context about your life and work. Your graph files stay on your device — Graphnosis has no servers. Recalled content passes through your AI client and is subject to its privacy policy.

Every memory is encrypted at rest with a passphrase you control. Recall is deterministic and auditable. Sensitive memories are gated behind an in-app consent modal before any AI client can read them.

The 47 MCP tools cover the full memory lifecycle: save a note, recall by semantic search, correct a stored fact, explore your knowledge graph, train reusable SOPs (skills + goals), and run multi-step procedures grounded in your personal context.

cortex_path.title: Cortex folder
cortex_path.description: The folder where your Graphnosis cortex is stored. Open the Graphnosis app → Folder path is displayed on the status bar.

passphrase.title: Cortex passphrase
passphrase.description: The passphrase you set when you created your cortex. Graphnosis derives an Argon2id encryption key from it — the passphrase itself is never stored by Graphnosis. Stored securely by Claude Desktop; never logged or sent over the wire.

default_engram.title: Default engram
default_engram.description: The engram new memories are saved into when no topic is specified. Leave blank to use 'personal'.
